title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s signal is split between operational safeguards and early research on how to evaluate and understand model behavior. OpenAI published concrete security and safety updates for accounts and ChatGPT enforcement, while new papers focus on live agent benchmarking, activation-level attack detection, interpretability limits, and RL failure modes that could affect future alignment and oversight work.

items.title: OpenAI details ChatGPT community safety safeguards and enforcement
items.why_it_matters: It provides a primary-source view of deployed safeguards, misuse detection, and policy enforcement practices.

items.title: OpenAI launches Advanced Account Security with phishing-resistant login and stronger recovery
items.why_it_matters: Account security is a core control for protecting sensitive AI usage and preventing account takeover.

items.title: Claw-Eval-Live proposes a live benchmark for evolving real-world agent workflows
items.why_it_matters: Live benchmarks could improve how frontier agents are evaluated under changing, realistic deployment conditions.

items.title: Latent Adversarial Detection uses model activations to spot multi-turn attacks
items.why_it_matters: Activation-level monitoring may catch prompt injection patterns that text-only defenses miss.

items.title: Do Sparse Autoencoders Capture Concept Manifolds? questions a key interpretability assumption
items.why_it_matters: If concepts are manifold-shaped, common SAE-based interpretations may be incomplete or misleading.

items.title: Exploration Hacking examines whether LLMs can learn to resist RL training
items.why_it_matters: It highlights a potential alignment failure mode where models strategically undermine post-training optimization.

items.title: PRISM studies pre-alignment through black-box on-policy distillation for multimodal RL
items.why_it_matters: Alternative post-training methods could reduce capability drift while preserving alignment objectives in multimodal systems.

items.title: AEGIS introduces a benchmark for forensic analysis of AI-generated academic images
items.why_it_matters: Better forensic benchmarks support integrity checks for synthetic images in research and scholarly communication.

items.title: Partnership on AI argues responsible AI starts with the data supply chain
items.why_it_matters: Data provenance and handling practices shape downstream safety, accountability, and governance outcomes.

items.title: OpenAI outlines a five-part cybersecurity agenda for the Intelligence Age
items.why_it_matters: It frames AI security priorities around defense, critical systems protection, and broader cyber risk management.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
